title: Helsingin yliopisto päivittää ilmasto- ja luontotavoitteitaan
content:
University of Helsinki updates its climate and nature goals

jaskopra@helsinki.fi Wed, 08/04/2026 - 10:51
The University of Helsinki will update its carbon neutrality roadmap by 2030 roadmap to better meet the challenges of the climate and nature crisis and the requirements of the operating context. Work on the update will continue until 2027, with the University devoting more attention to the impacts of its operations.

title: Helsingin yliopiston farmasian tiedekunta sijoittui erinomaisesti QS-ainekohtaisessa yliopistovertailussa 2026
content:
University of Helsinki Pharmacy Achieves Strong Results in QS Subject Rankings 2026

lindymar@helsinki.fi Tue, 07/04/2026 - 09:43
The University of Helsinki has delivered a strong overall performance in the QS World University Rankings by Subject 2026, ranking among the top 100 globally in nearly twenty subjects - more than any other university in Finland.

Most notably, the Faculty of Pharmacy has been ranked 50th worldwide in Pharmacy.</

title: Arkadiankadun siltaremontti aiheuttaa muutoksia liikennejärjestelyihin Luonnontieteellisen museon lähistöllä
content:
Renewal of Arkadiankatu Bridge will cause changes to traffic arrangements near the Natural History Museum

johanemi@helsinki.fi Wed, 07/01/2026 - 08:40
The renovation is expected to be completed in late 2026.

The renovation will start with the construction of temporary traffic arrangements from Wednesday 7 January onwards. The renovation is expected to be completed in late 2026.

title: Kotina metsä -näyttely tuo luonnonmetsän keskelle Helsinkiä
content:
Forest Home – Exhibition brings a natural forest to the heart of Helsinki

johanemi@helsinki.fi Fri, 30/05/2025 - 14:09
In Finland, natural forests have become a rarity – and for many, they remain unfamiliar. The Forest Home exhibition invites visitors to discover them through photography, video installations and scientific specimens. Forest Home is on display at the Natural History Museum until 5 April 2026.
